title: Nostra Research Space Ontology
description: Example Space-local ontology for research planning, hypotheses, and experiment evidence.

classes.description: Sovereign container for policies, contributions, and knowledge bundles.
Governed unit of knowledge, work, or evidence that can be linked and versioned.
Typed link between two governed graph subjects.
A declared ability or feature exposed by a Space or contribution surface.
Named graph scope used to partition evidence and traversal.
A research claim or question that can be tested and refined.
classes.notes: Space-local additive extension for research programs.

classes.description: A concrete execution of a research method that produces evidence.
classes.notes: Represents a governed experiment pass or evaluation run.

relations.description: A Contribution relates to another Contribution.
A Contribution is scoped by a ProvenanceScope.
An experiment run tests a research hypothesis.
relations.notes: Space-local relation layered on top of the core graph.

relations.description: A contribution provides evidence for a hypothesis.

properties.description: Canonical portable identifier for a graph subject or relation.
Authority ceiling for a bundled artifact or query.
Optional scope-specific reference required operationally for actor and agent scopes.
A bounded confidence score attached to a hypothesis or experiment result.
The research method or protocol used by an experiment run.

extension_rules: Research Space terms must remain namespace-prefixed under research: and additive to the core ontology.
Research Space extensions must not redefine Space, Contribution, Relation, Capability, or ProvenanceScope.
Breaking changes to research-space semantics require steward review and an explicit compatibility note.
Research Space extensions must not add new provenance scope classes or values.
